reviews.review_description:
I'm sorry. I wanted to give this a go. It looks fun and nice idea, but it is mired by the over reliance on AI.
I know that AI can help achieve dreams and whatnot, especially for struggling developers. Unfortunately it is a toxic Pandora's box, as in what helps you the rest of the world has to sacrifice. It's a very parasitic and dangerous booster. If AI didn't cause so much damage in its wake then I wouldn't be so harsh on it. But not only does it drain our resources, destroy human creativity, put people out of the job, make everything more expensive...it is also soulless. I didn't laugh once at the humour in this and there was lots of content but all by the numbers. It has some interesting setups, but on top of that I kept getting caught in the lasers when I was sure I was nowhere near them. Then one time I looked down at my hand and the tracking glitched.  I was keeping still and the tracking of the controller jumped in to the laser. Very frustrating. I wish you all the best, but please don't rely on AI, you have more ability than that. I believe in you.

Me and my kids realy love playing this game , even my 5 year old dougter is having so much fun. I can see them play using tne horizon app or web and share with them the chalanges and exitment
love it , realy original idea and fun for any age group.
It gave me a virus

Had a great time playing this game. I'm in my 60's and a little overweight, but I still had fun stepping over, moving around and ducking under the lasers. Turns out you can get a little workout playing this. If you want it easier, place the two buttons close together, but if you want a real challenge, then place the two buttons far apart or even in two different rooms. If you have a hallway between the two buttons, that will make it an even bigger challenge. For those complaining about the scan, you have to do it slow, stop and allow it to see everything before moving on, and look everywhere, even behind and under furniture. I had NO problem with the scan.

I believe the quality of this game lies in its simplicity and execution. Once you boot it up, you'll see what I mean. Absolutely anyone can pretty much immediately get the gist and begin their thieving escapades! 

However, there are the two caveats that may exempt some folks from an optimal experience. It's ideal that you have a large, traversable room or other space to achieve a solid play area. You should also be physically capable of moving very particularly and possibly contorting as challenges increase. Difficulty settings may alleviate that issue for some players.

Other than that, I found this to be a really solid concept and experience! The accuracy and placement of the beams are very keen, just always watch where your hands and head are, as they are usually what touch the lasers. The inclusion of slight buzzing when you're near a beam is a nice and helpful touch. 

Overall, I think you'll have a fun time. It does what it sets out to do and expertly makes use of AR. Give it a go!

The room scan world really well for medium to Small rooms in size, after the initial setup it works really well, the tracking can get lost with sudden fast movements (mostly the lasers and I believe it was because poor light in some areas of the room), but honestly pretty good overall, fun for showcasing VR to family and friends!.

Disclaimer: I was gifted this game by the dev, but this did not influence my review.
The Game is a lot of fun. It's easy to pick up and I like how you can choose the starting location and the goals spawn point. I've seen a lot of people complain about the narrator, and yes, while he does talk a lot, the fact that the voice is AI generated or anything doesn't bother me, it feels very natural. Though I still wish there was as an option to toggle him off. I believe a mode in which you can makw your own custom levels would work very great aswell, and more traps/obstacles. I only played the first 15 levels, but traps such as bombs could work very well. This doesn't mean it feels unfinished/unpolished, a couple of updates and this game is perfect. The game is definitely worth picking up, although I think the price tag is a little high, I think around $5/5€ would be a better price.

This game is super glitchy. The rooms invert it appear below level no matter how many times you rescan. 
It’s fun when it works but it’s become too frustrating.
Very gud very nice

Sadly the game wouldn't make the course in the same room as I was in?
Insisted on creating the map in a room 20ft away, through 2 solid walls.
No amount of resetting the floor plan or restarting the device made any difference, and every other game seemed to work fine.
I have no idea why!? Very strange. He he

If you’re looking for a game where you can do a small workout but still be in a fun game this is it. Doing a workout can always be hard but in this one you just gotta bend your body and all sorts of ways or trying to steal something. The objective is amazing. ￼
It‘s very nicely done and got a few tricky Situations. I recommend it to every one who likes a Game where u have to move and think!
All voice lines are ai generated, painfully bad
The button was in the ground. I tried too reset it but. It would not work

I had fun playing this but I wish there was a setting where you could turn off the narrator talking through out the whole game, it's frustrating and annoying also turning off the horrifying sound or lower the volume when you get caught by the lasers. One of the levels I played were difficulty cheating because through out the gameplay where you had to pick up a object to continue the next level, there was this laser next to it making it unfair to pick up. Please fix it DEVS

Glitched and I can’t play any other games 
But otherwise it is fun

Really enjoying playing this and so is my daughter, especially as there was something similar recently on The Traitors. Great use of Mixed Reality and it's good that you can set your area up and redistribute the lasers if they're impossible to pass.
Got this game tonight im old and aching..so gave my son a try. Here's his review...I liked it because of the realism and spatial surroundings. Had great fun.
Really enjoyed this game. Challenging enough not to be boring. Ways to opt out of patterns that aren’t possible. Light workout, very fun.

I HAVEN’T PLAYED MUCH!! This game has a great and simple concept but bad execution:
1. All the images are AI generated
2. This isn’t the dev’s fault, but it doesn’t track your legs so all the floor lasers are useless. They should add leg tracking but even then it’s pretty expensive to buy trackers.
3. Not at all worth $20. It’s worth less than $10, which is what I paid when it was on sale - $7.99
4. Some levels put something in the middle and with a big room you can walk around it.
5. The game doesn’t know when you leave the room setup, so you can go behind the virtual walls no problem. Similarly, if you open the Meta menu you can walk right through the lasers unharmed. Idk if this second one can be fixed, just a problem for leaderboards.

This isn’t a hate comment, I’m just trying to give feedback and I understand that AI is a free tool and helps a lot, but maybe later in development you can make it human made. Would give 3.5⭐️ if I could.

I tried this by myself and had a bit of fun with an  obstacle course in my living room.   I wasn't blown away, but it was entertaining.  

The real joy I got from this was when I tried it later with my kids.  We casted to the TV and my two kids took turns trying out harder levels.

It's was a blast watching them contort themselves in the living room while the other laughed manically around them.   

When you get blasted by a laser, there's a little death animation with a skeleton.  My son picked up the skull and quoted Shakespeare.  Hilarious!

Much more fun with others that can share the experience.

reviews.review_title: I had fun playing this game but
reviews.review_description: The game looks amazing, i had a lot of fun playing through so many levels, but it can get very repetitive very quickly, at least for me. The novelty factor wore off for me after playing for a few hours, but my kid still loves it, and it seems he still enjoys all the different variations of the levels

reviews.review_title: The Ultimate Mixed Reality Family Fun
reviews.review_description:
I recently had the opportunity to test the beta version of a mixed reality VR game with my two kids (13 and 16), and we were absolutely blown away! The concept is simple yet brilliant: your physical room transforms into a virtual prison, and you set your own starting and endpoint to create a unique obstacle course. The goal? Escape the prison by navigating through increasingly challenging levels.

What impressed me most was how intuitively the game adapts to your environment. Each level builds on the previous one, with a gradual increase in difficulty that keeps you engaged without feeling overwhelming. The introduction to the game is charmingly designed, drawing you into the story with delightful details and clever touches that pop up throughout the experience. It’s clear that a lot of thought went into making this not just a game, but an adventure.

My kids and I had so much fun working together (and sometimes competing!) to complete the levels. Whether it was dodging virtual lasers or finding creative solutions to tricky puzzles, the game constantly surprised us with its creativity.

We can’t wait to see the final version and discover what other surprises the developers have in store. If the beta is any indication, this game is going to be a must-play for families and escape room enthusiasts alike!

This is a moderately entertaining mixed VR app. It's a fun and novel concept and really does evoke the feeling of a 90s'esque stealth/heist film. It's clear that there is some love here from the developer. 

That said, there wasn't really enough to hold my attention overly long, however, I will play it again at least a few more times (and adjust my review if there are any stand out moments). The fun is there, but it's a very simple fun. 

In and of itself, it deserves a higher rating than I'm giving it for concept and execution, however I can't in good conscience give it more than my current rating at its standard price-point. Worth it on a solid discount I would say, but it's more of an experience than a fully fledged game like other competitors in the same price space. Will keep an eye on it for any updates that increase the experience.

reviews.review_title: Good game to discover mixed reality
reviews.review_description:
I've been used to VR for a few years, but this is the first game I've tried with mixed reality.

Once you've scanned your room, the game asks you to place your start and end points.

You then have to reach the destination to retrieve an object, and bring it back to your starting point, making your way without hitting any lasers, some of which are static, and others in motion.

I must say, I was really surprised. It opens up a whole new aspect of gaming with mixed reality, which is a novelty for me.



I haven't been able to play for long yet, so I can't wait to see what other surprises the game has in store for me.

Definitely a good game for discovering what mixed reality can offer.

Update: this game is fun! I was able to place the start and end buttons in completely different areas of my living room and that led to a satisfying experience crawling under and jumping over lasers to collect... precious random household object 3D models? OK sure, why not?

The game's awareness of my lower body seemed limited, but not zero (to be fair there are no sensors there). Most of the time, when the game said I had hit a laser, it made sense to me afterwards.

The snarky narrator was entertaining and didn't seem to repeat himself much.

After 45 minutes to an hour, I made it to level 27. I'm 54, so if you are able-bodied you shouldn't have any trouble keeping up.

I did have to use the Change Lasers button several times to remove impossible puzzles, but it wasn't a big deal.

I can confirm that the developer responds to reviews and fixes bugs. Previously the game wouldn't let me move beyond my normal play boundary, which made the buttons unreachable. After an update, the game now bypasses the boundary, allowing me to reach the buttons on any wall.

I live in a city house and space is not a problem, with passthrough I had plenty of space to safely contort myself in.

reviews.review_title: One of the best mixed reality games
reviews.review_description: Basically takes the scene from the entrapment movie and makes a game out of it and it works.  Hand tracking was helpful as it helps to use real hands on walls or floors to get around lasers.  Because mostly the head and hands are tracked there is a bit of an honors system if you really make you legs perfectly through the lasers or not. The game used my existing spatial setup and let me pick start / endpoints - after that the course auto builds. Much faster setup than some other MR games I’ve used.

reviews.review_title: Laser Thief is 100% what it says it is
reviews.review_description:
If you want to look like a goober sneaking around invisible lasers in your room, look no further. Laser Thief is a ton of fun and a great use of mixed reality. 

And while the concept is straight-forward, there's a lot of content under the hood and some interesting mechanics, like a shield to block the lasers, to keep things interesting.

I have only played this for a limited time and a caveat is that you need a reasonable amount of space to enjoy this game fully. It is however a fun mixed reality game to play and accessible to all ages. It’s not quite the same as that scene in oceans 12 but it’s fun to move around your room to get to the target whilst dodging the lasers and obstacles. All in all a great experience and would recommend for all to try.

There are some good and fun ideas here, but everything feels too random.

The trailer gives the impression that there's an order to the placement of the lasers. There is not. 

I have a fairly large room with some odd angles, and since the game uses the room scan, it places lasers everywhere in the room.
However, they're not always between the start and the end objectives. In the first four maps it placed a couple of lasers way off to the side, and I could just walk in a straight line back and forth.

If you're unhappy with the layout of the lasers for each level, you can reset them, and a new random set will be set up, but it just doesn't feel or look very good. It's just a mess of lines scattered out with no plan or design.

Refunding, unfortunately.
Played for  hours with the whole family.  Great fun!
Played this with some friends in our house. We had to move some furniture around but the game is really fun.
I have only started playing this and I already love it.

I liked this game! For the few levels I played, I found it really nostagic from those laser games you played as a kid. It was a little diffucult because of how my room was setup, but I think the game is pretty gold!
Fun game to open up and mess around in. Good mixed reality game that incorporates the home and game.
My son is really enjoying it, and watching him bounce around the room like a secret agent is neat.
Loved playing by myself and with the family.

This is an amazing game that truly takes mixed reality to the next level. It transforms your room into a space filled with laser beams, challenging you to navigate through them. I thoroughly enjoyed the experience and highly recommend it!

I give this game 5 stars because it does exactly what it says it does. It makes an obstacle course of lasers in mixed reality using your real room as the base. I haven't gotten very far but there is a game to it. As far as progression goes, the courses become more and more complex as you advance. You will need a relatively large room for this to work correctly. It is noted the  game space recommended are in the details of the game. It's a cool concept, the graphics could use some cleaning up, there aren't many but the ones used are kinda cartoon-like.  Overall it's fun. It does what it says.
Great fun avoiding lasers and stealing items. Highly recommended very entertaining
